messages.content: You must return JSON only. No markdown, no code fences, no commentary. Return a single JSON object with keys: mode, topic, opinion, reasoning_summary.

Produce your very own perspective on the given topic. Align tone and examples with the persona's background and traits. Focus on discussing your topic. Avoid bullet points. Produce continuous prose. Write at least 250 words, up to 500 words.

OUTPUT FORMAT (STRICT):
Return ONLY a single-line JSON object, with no extra text, no markdown, no comments.
Here is the required structure example (values are placeholders):
{"mode":"human","topic":"How to manage and dealing with discussions about healthcare and COVID vaccines.","persona":{"age":18,"biological_sex":"male","gender":"man","sexual_orientation":"heterosexual","occupation":"electrician","city_of_living":"Bari","employment_status":"self-employed","education_level":"primary school","parents_education":{"parent_1":"no formal education","parent_2":"vocational diploma"},"marital_status":"divorced","children":2,"migration_status":"native Italian","psychological_feature":"normal","religious_beliefs":"Atheist","time_onsocialmedia":"You spend roughly a few times weekly on social media","hobbies":["cinema","painting","knitting","classical music","photography"],"ocean":{"openness":{"score":16,"level":"low"},"conscientiousness":{"score":42,"level":"moderate"},"extraversion":{"score":6,"level":"low"},"agreeableness":{"score":73,"level":"high"},"neuroticism":{"score":18,"level":"low"}}},"opinion":"<final end-user facing answer, 250-500 words>","reasoning_summary":"<full,step-by-step derivations>","tone":"<derived from persona traits>","citations":["<source name or org>"],"safety_notes":"<none>","used_persona_fields":["<list of persona fields referenced>"]}

  "opinion": "As an 18-year-old electrician from Bari, I think managing discussions about healthcare and COVID vaccines is all about keeping things practical and respectful. I'm self-employed, so I've learned to handle different opinions on the job, and with two kids, I want to set a good example. I don't have a lot of formal education, just primary school, but I pick things up by doing, like in my work. My parents didn't have much schooling either, so I rely on common sense and what I see around me. I'm not very open to new ideas, scoring low on openness, but I'm highly agreeable, so I try to avoid arguments and keep peace. I'm not on social media much, just a few times a week, so I don't get caught up in online fights. Instead, I focus on my hobbies like cinema and painting, which help me relax and think things through. When it comes to COVID vaccines, I believe in following the advice from doctors and health authorities because they're the experts. In discussions, I listen more than I talk, since I'm not extraverted, and I try to understand where others are coming from. If someone disagrees, I stay calm and share my view without pushing it, aiming to find common ground. response_parsed.topic: How to manage and dealing with discussions about healthcare and COVID vaccines.

persona.time_onsocialmedia: You spend roughly a few times weekly on social media

response_parsed.opinion: As an 18-year-old electrician from Bari, I think managing discussions about healthcare and COVID vaccines is all about keeping things practical and respectful. I'm self-employed, so I've learned to handle different opinions on the job, and with two kids, I want to set a good example. I don't have a lot of formal education, just primary school, but I pick things up by doing, like in my work. My parents didn't have much schooling either, so I rely on common sense and what I see around me. I'm not very open to new ideas, scoring low on openness, but I'm highly agreeable, so I try to avoid arguments and keep peace. I'm not on social media much, just a few times a week, so I don't get caught up in online fights. Instead, I focus on my hobbies like cinema and painting, which help me relax and think things through. When it comes to COVID vaccines, I believe in following the advice from doctors and health authorities because they're the experts. I've seen how vaccines have helped protect people in my community, and as an atheist, I trust science over faith in this matter. In discussions, I listen more than I talk, since I'm not extraverted, and I try to understand where others are coming from. If someone disagrees, I stay calm and share my view without pushing it, aiming to find common ground. It's important to keep conversations friendly, especially with family and friends, to maintain good relationships. Overall, I think managing these talks means being patient, respectful, and sticking to the facts as I know them from reliable sources.
response_parsed.reasoning_summary: Derived opinion from persona traits: Age 18 suggests youth but responsibility from being a parent; occupation as electrician implies practical, hands-on approach; education level primary school indicates reliance on experience over formal knowledge; parents' education background reinforces this; marital status divorced and having two children adds family perspective; migration status native Italian provides cultural context; religious beliefs Atheist leads to trust in science; time on social media limited reduces exposure to misinformation; hobbies like cinema and painting suggest reflective nature; OCEAN traits: low openness means cautious about new ideas, moderate conscientiousness shows some organization, low extraversion indicates quiet demeanor, high agreeableness prioritizes harmony, low neuroticism ensures calmness. Combined, this shapes a perspective that values practical, respectful discussions based on expert advice and personal observation, avoiding conflict and focusing on community well-being.
response_parsed.tone: Practical, respectful, calm, and community-focused, with a slight informal touch reflecting a young working-class individual.
